reason: Quality review should account for cross-layer and reuse risks.
Review must verify schedule/status data is consistent from database through UI.
Review must catch duplicated helpers or missed existing patterns.
Backend route, service, migration, ownership, and external API behavior need spec review.
Review must verify non-destructive migrations and data model consistency.
Review must verify dispatch failures and relogin flows surface correctly.
Backend quality rules apply to publishing services and tests.
Review must verify publishing platform contracts for server-owned dispatch, timer format, grouping, status mapping, QR sanitization, and ownership.
Publishing workspace UI needs frontend spec review.
Review must verify new UI follows component conventions.
Review must verify query/mutation and server state patterns.
Review must verify plan editor state is not tangled with server status.
Review must verify strong API and form typing.
Review must verify UI quality, responsive behavior, and user-facing copy rules.
Shared publishing contracts should be checked for export consistency.
Review must verify shared publishing types stay type-safe.
Review must verify implementation matches dispatch API status, relogin, and timer format constraints.
